definitions: In this section an overview will be given of the used terms and definition specifically of this product.
service_summary:
This section gives a brief summary of the described Service.
The Service covers all tasks and activities required to deliver the Shared NAS Service. Through this managed service offering, Cegeka aims to relieve the Customer of the technical responsibilities associated with managing and operating complex NAS infrastructure.
The standard Service features are designed to ensure the platform supporting the Shared NAS Service remains operational, secure, and technically optimized.
services:
The Shared NAS Service provides a network-attached storage platform that delivers general-purpose file services within Cegeka's Cloud infrastructure.
It operates on dedicated hardware that runs its own operating system and offers a range of integrated capabilities. The service includes the use of storage capacity as well as several key features, including MetroMirror, synchronous and asynchronous replication, enterprise WORM functionality, snapshot capabilities, antivirus and auditing integrations, and built-in ransomware protection. Together, these capabilities ensure a reliable, secure, and versatile file-storage environment for customer workloads.
standard services:
This section describes the activities which are executed as part of the the Service.
Technical Management
Cegeka is responsible for the technical management required to deliver the Shared NAS Service and for ensuring its proper functioning during the agreed availability windows.
Technical management of the Shared NAS Service includes the following core (platform) activities:
Objective: delivering and operating the Shared NAS Service
Objective: ensuring the NAS platform stays current, supported, and compliant
Objective: protecting, recovering, and securing stored data
Objective: ensuring the Shared NAS Service has sufficient, reliable, and optimised resources
Objective: ensuring secure and compliant management of the platform
Security and Compliance
Security and compliance are central to any Service delivered by Cegeka. Cegeka ensures that all infrastructure components are deployed, configured, and maintained in line with recognized security standards and agreed contractual requirements. The goal is to protect the infrastructure component and its data while aligning with both Cegeka’s internal security practices and the Customer’s specific policies.
For this particular Service, the supporting platform -based on NetApp technology- has been configured follwing NetApp's security hardening guidelines, which provides a set of controls allowing hardening of the ONTAP storage operating system.
Security incident management is performed in accordance with Cegeka’s internal policies as well as the contractual security requirements of the Customer. When a potential breach is detected, a security incident ticket is created and communicated without delay to both the Customer and the relevant Cegeka stakeholders.
Privileged access for Cegeka engineering teams, as well as privileged access for customers or third parties to the platform delivering the Shared NAS Service is carried out by Cegeka in close collaboration with the Customer. Account management follows the agreed Customer’s identity and access management (IAM) policies to ensure secure and compliant access practices.
Patch Management
Effective patch management is critical for keeping systems up to date, minimizing the attack surface, and ensuring that the infrastructure, operating system, applications, etc... remain fully supported. This section outlines the patch policy governing the service and describes the specific approach applied to each component.
Regular patching is a fundamental element of the Service’s operational management. High-risk vulnerabilities requiring urgent remediation will be addressed outside the standard patching cycle through expedited updates.
Upgrades are performed only when recommended by the vendor, based on internal metrics such as run hours, version stability, and identified defects. The following sections describe the patching policy applicable to this service:
OS patching
Potential operating system upgrades for storage systems are evaluated on a quarterly basis. Upgrades are typically scheduled when at least one of the following conditions is met: an important security patch is required to mitigate a vulnerability; a critical stability update is needed to resolve functional issues or performance degradation; or a new feature, available only in a more recent OS version, is actively required.
In addition to this periodic review and planning process, the Storage team may also initiate an ad-hoc OS upgrade if specific threats or issues arise that impact the quality or reliability of the service delivery.
Firmware updates
Firmware updates for components such as drives, drive shelves, and switches are performed in conjunction with operating system upgrades. An exception is made only when an ad-hoc firmware update is deemed urgently necessary due to specific threats or issues identified that may affect the quality or stability of the service.
Application updates
Updates for dependent applications, such as the ONTAP Mediator, are performed alongside OS upgrades when recommended by the vendor. An exception applies only when an ad-hoc update is deemed urgently required due to specific threats or issues identified that could impact the quality or continuity of the service.
Monitoring & backup
As outlined in Chapter 2.3.2 "Prerequisites & Conditions", adoption of the Shared NAS Service is exclusively available to customers with existing environments and workloads, hosted within the premises of Cegeka's Cloud infrastructure. These services provide the essential supporting components -such as the monitoring and backup- required to enable the activities provided by the Shared NAS Service. This can be summarised as follows:
Note: costs related to backup is invoiced separately.
optional services:
This section outlines the optional services which may be added to the list of standard services and -when applicable- will be priced in addition to the standard offering.
The following options can be enabled, yet require a technical review prior to activation:
Activation of any optional service feature requires the creation of an RFC, which will be handled in accordance with the standard change process.
requests:
The handling of requests -either standard or non-standard- is subject to the regular change management process (see Enabling Services). Standard Change Requests Standard change requests related to the Service, can be requested when available. A standard change request is a pre-approved and pre-defined service request, often used for recurring needs that arise multiple times per month. All standard change requests are processed as change tickets and are delivered within the indicated lead time. Wherever possible, the execution of these requests is optimally automated, ensuring efficiency and consistency. Standard change requests are offered at a fixed price. | Name | LEAD TIME (*) | | Extend an existing share | 1 business days | | Add a share (on an existing NAS server) | 1 business day | | Remove a share | 1 business day | | New NAS server | 2 business days | | Remove NAS server (decommission) | 1 business day | | Enable Antivirus integration for NAS serve | 1 business day | | Enable Ransomware Protection for NAS server | 1 business day | | Enable audit logging for NAS server | 1 business day |
Name LEAD TIME (*) Extend an existing share 1 business days Add a share (on an existing NAS server) 1 business day Remove a share 1 business day New NAS server 2 business days Remove NAS server (decommission) 1 business day Enable Antivirus integration for NAS serve 1 business day Enable Ransomware Protection for NAS server 1 business day Enable audit logging for NAS server 1 business day * estimated lead time applies from the moment the change request has been received by the delivery team. Non-standard Change Requests Next to Standard Change Requests, the Service also facilitates and supports non-standard change requests when applicable. Non-standard change requests are requests which are not pre-approved nor pre-defined and therefore require a thorough evaluation and analysis. In such cases, Cegeka prepares a proposal along with an impact assessment and a time-and-materials (T&M) estimation to address the request. Before execution, the proposal must undergo review, approval, and planning by the Change Advisory Board (CAB), including the scheduling of an appropriate maintenance window. Non-standard change requests are billed on a T&M basis.
out_of_scope: Everything that is not explicitly mentioned in this service description is by default out of scope.
obligatory: This section outlines the additional services that must be in place for Cegeka to effectively deliver the described Service.
conditions:
This section defines the conditions that must be fulfilled for Cegeka to deliver the described Service effectively.
Prerequisites :
The Shared NAS Service is only available to customers with existing environments and workloads, hosted within the premises of Cegeka's Cloud infrastructure.
Conditions :
cegeka owned licenses and/or subscriptio:
The Service and its corresponding pricing model, includes all required licenses to operate the platform in compliance with the vendors licensing model. This includes licenses required to enable specific features and/or functionality (eg. Autonomous Ransomware Protection)
It is Cegeka’s responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. Licenses and/or subscriptions used for Enabling Services are included in the Service and are not listed below.
When Cegeka is responsible for maintaining license and/or subscription compliance, the following related administrative activities are provided and included in the Service :
customer_licenses:
When applicable - Licenses and/or subscriptions owned by the Customer, as well as their associated costs, are not included in this Service. In this scenario, the Customer is responsible for ensuring compliance with all applicable licenses and subscriptions.
To support this, the Customer must provide Cegeka with the relevant license information. Cegeka will assist in maintaining compliance by sharing expertise, reporting on license usage and requirements.
N/A for this Service as all required platform licenses are owned by Cegeka (cfr. 2.3.3)
location:
This section mentions the location from which this service is delivered.
This Service is delivered remotely from the Cegeka (virtual) office environment.
ownership:
This section defines the ownership of the equipment related to the service.
N/A for this Service as underlying infrastructure equipment is owned by Cegeka
misc:
In this section specific topics regarding the Service will be listed and shortly described.
Data Access and Confidentiality :
Cegeka does not require access to confidential data in order to deliver this Service. The credentials used by Cegeka engineers do not provide access to such data for the purpose of this Service. In the event of functional issue investigations, Cegeka personnel may incidentally view or receive fragments of such data if it is shared by the Customer (eg. exports, screensharing or screenshots).
Service Reporting :
Monthly reporting will be provided to confirm service availability, demonstrate adherence to SLAs, and deliver insights into current capacity usage and anticipated expansion requirements.
Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer:
This service is delivered with:

technical details: The following section provides more technical details about the underlying platform in support of the described Shared NAS Service.
autonomous ransomware protection:
Autonomous Ransomware Protection (ARP) is a native ONTAP capability designed to provide secure, built-in data protection at the storage layer. It delivers real-time detection and response by monitoring file extensions, data entropy, and IOPS patterns. Through AI-driven analysis, it can also identify sophisticated ransomware techniques, such as header manipulation or partial file-level encryption, which are increasingly common in newer attack variants.
When ARP detects suspicious activity, it automatically creates an immutable snapshot. Such activities trigger alerts which -when integrated with Cegeka's Modern SOC- are forwarded our SOC analysts for further assessment.
All ARP-triggered snapshots are immutable (ref. 6.1.1.1) and cannot be removed, ensuring that the protected data remains unaltered and undeletable, even by administrators. This guarantees a dependable recovery point in the event of an actual attack.
The AI model powering ARP is pre-trained externally from ONTAP and is continuously updated by NetApp to maintain the effectiveness of its detection capabilities.
6.1.1.1 Immutable Snapshot Schedule
The immutable snapshot schedule serves as a proactive safeguard, enabling rapid recovery in situations where ransomware activity may have altered files without being detected by the ARP. Users can restore affected files directly through the 'previous versions'- feature, eliminating the need to perform a restore from backup.
Customers can select from three predefined snapshot schedules:
